### Fix 3

Rule [sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-u-color-200]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-u-color-200' was removed in the new version. A simple version prefix swap to 'pf-v6-u-color-200' does NOT produce a valid class — this class does not exist in the target CSS distribution.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a PatternFly component prop.

Code context:
```

  392              <span className="pf-v5-u-font-size-sm pf-v5-u-color-200">
  393                {t('Generated on')}: {tokenGenerationTimestamp}

```

### Fix 4
Lines: 222, 232, 417
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: FlexItem

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Button
Prop: icon

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 222):
  221                <FlexItem>
  222                  <Button
  223                    key="cancel"

  (line 232):
  231                <FlexItem>
  232                  <Button
  233                    key="save"

  (line 417):
  416        <FlexItem>
  417          <Button
  418            type="button"

```

### Fix 5
Line: 392
Rule [semver-css-class-removed-u-color-200]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-u-color-200' has no equivalent in the new version. There is no valid 'pf-v6-u-color-200' class — this class was removed, not renamed.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a component prop.

Incident context:
  matchingText: pf-v5-u-font-size-sm pf-v5-u-color-200

Code context:
```
  391            <LevelItem>

  393                {t('Generated on')}: {tokenGenerationTimestamp}

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/odf-console/source/packages/odf/components/storage-consumers/onboarding-modal.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the file to understand its current state.

Now let me analyze each fix:

**Fix 1** (Lines 224, 236, 420): `Button.variant` type changed — a `'stateful'` variant was added. The existing values `secondary` and `primary` are still valid. **No change needed.**

**Fix 2** (Line 407): `TextArea.resizeOrientation` type changed — a `'none'` value was added. The existing value `"vertical"` is still valid. **No change needed.**

**Fix 3** (Line 392): CSS class `pf-v5-u-color-200` was removed. Need to remove this class. Looking at line 392, it currently reads `"pf-v6-u-font-size-sm pf-v5-u-color-200"`. The `pf-v5-u-color-200` class is removed and has no replacement. I need to remove it, leaving just `"pf-v6-u-font-size-sm"`.

**Fix 4** (Lines 222, 232, 417): Icon children in `<Button>` should be moved to the `icon` prop.
- Line 222-229: `<Button>` with text `{t('Cancel')}` — no icon children. **No change needed.**
- Line 232-242: `<Button>` with text `{inProgress ? t('Generating token') : t('Generate token')}` — no icon children. **No change needed.**
- Line 417-425: `<Button>` has `<CopyIcon>` AND text `{t('Copy to clipboard')}`. The icon needs to move to the `icon` prop.

**Fix 5** (Line 392): Same as Fix 3 — CSS class `pf-v5-u-color-200` removed. Already handled.

Let me apply the changes:

arguments.after:
<Button
          type="button"
          onClick={onCopyToClipboard}
          variant={ButtonVariant.primary}
          className="odf-onboarding-modal__clipboard"
          icon={<CopyIcon className="pf-v6-u-ml-md pf-v6-u-mr-sm" />}
        >
          {t('Copy to clipboard')}
        </Button>
arguments.before:
<Button
          type="button"
          onClick={onCopyToClipboard}
          variant={ButtonVariant.primary}
          className="odf-onboarding-modal__clipboard"
        >
          <CopyIcon className="pf-v6-u-ml-md pf-v6-u-mr-sm" />
          {t('Copy to clipboard')}
        </Button>

content.text: Both changes look correct. Let me also verify the imports are still consistent (CopyIcon is still used in the `icon` prop, so it's still needed):

Good — `CopyIcon` is still referenced so the import is still needed. All changes are clean.

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Rule | Status | Details |
|-----|------|--------|---------|
| **Fix 1** | `Button.variant` type changed | **Already valid — no change needed** | The `variant` values used (`ButtonVariant.secondary`, `ButtonVariant.primary`) remain valid in the new type. The only change was adding `'stateful'` as a new option. |
| **Fix 2** | `TextArea.resizeOrientation` type changed | **Already valid — no change needed** | The value `"vertical"` remains valid in the new type. The only change was adding `'none'` as a new option. |
| **Fix 3** | CSS class `pf-v5-u-color-200` removed (dead class) | **Applied** | Removed `pf-v5-u-color-200` from the `className` on line 392, leaving `"pf-v6-u-font-size-sm"`. This class was removed with no v6 equivalent. |
| **Fix 4** | Icon children to `icon` prop on `<Button>` | **Partially applied** | Line 417: Moved `<CopyIcon className="pf-v6-u-ml-md pf-v6-u-mr-sm" />` from Button children to the `icon` prop. Lines 222 and 232: No change needed — those Buttons contain only text children, no icon elements. |
| **Fix 5** | CSS class `pf-v5-u-color-200` removed (semver rule) | **Already applied by Fix 3** | Same line 392, same class — handled by Fix 3. |
